---
title: Workspace management
description: Manage projects, repositories, sync policies, and activity from one Settings hub.
---

Kiket consolidates operational management under **Settings** so teams do not have to jump between scattered dialogs.

## Canonical sections

Use these routes as the source of truth for management workflows:

- **Projects**: `/settings/projects`
- **Repositories**: `/settings/repositories`
- **Sync Policies**: `/settings/sync`
- **History & Activity**: `/settings/history`

## Projects (`/settings/projects`)

Use this section for full project lifecycle operations:

- create new projects
- edit project name/description
- delete projects (with confirmation)
- view linked repository context

Project creation uses the same source model as onboarding:

- **Copy from Template**
- **Use Existing Repo**
- **Create New Repo**

## Repositories (`/settings/repositories`)

Repository management covers:

- connected repo inventory
- project-to-repo linking
- branch visibility and repository metadata
- connect/disconnect actions

## Sync Policies (`/settings/sync`)

Sync controls are centralized here:

- `syncMode`: `webhook` / `scheduled` / `manual`
- `syncIntervalMinutes` for scheduled repositories
- branch updates
- manual sync trigger with status feedback

## History & Activity (`/settings/history`)

The timeline combines management events from multiple systems:

- audit log events (project/repository mutations and other settings actions)
- repository sync execution history
- filters by action/resource/status and text search
- export actions for compliance and reporting

## Recommended team workflow

1. Create or connect workspaces in **Projects** and **Repositories**.
2. Define sync behavior in **Sync Policies**.
3. Verify outcomes and incidents in **History & Activity**.

This keeps day-2 operations predictable and makes ownership clear across admins, operators, and engineering leads.
